operating_state: Total production reached 1,754 MBOED in Q3 2022, increasing 210 MBOED year-over-year driven by new wells online across Lower 48, Alaska, Australia and China, plus Shell Permian acquisition and APLNG working interest expansion. Lower 48 achieved milestone production exceeding 1,000 MBOED while completing planned maintenance turnarounds.
cost_structure: Production and operating expenses increased driven by higher production volumes and inflationary impacts. DD&A expenses rose from higher production volumes, partially offset by lower rates from price-related reserve revisions. Environmental accrual decreased to $182 million with energy transition activities targeting Scope 1 and 2 emissions reduction projects allocated in capital budget.
material_events: Distributed $4.3 billion to shareholders through ordinary dividend, VROC and share repurchases while increasing quarterly dividend 11% to 51 cents per share and raising share repurchase authorization by $20 billion. Executed $3 billion debt reduction transactions and completed strategic asset transactions. ConocoPhillips joined OGMP 2.0 methane emissions reporting initiative and published Plan for the Net-Zero Energy Transition establishing 40-50% operational GHG emissions intensity reduction targets by 2030.
macro_exposures: Commodity price volatility continues with oil prices decreasing due to macroeconomic concerns while natural gas prices increased amid Ukraine conflict impacts and global supply disruptions. Permian basin production approaching offtake capacity limits causing higher regional differentials, expected to normalize as pipeline infrastructure completes. Company faces ongoing legal proceedings related to royalty underpayments, environmental contamination claims, and climate change litigation.
forward_outlook: Fourth-quarter 2022 production expected 1.74-1.80 MMBOED with operating capital guidance increased to $8.1 billion reflecting inflationary impacts and partner-operated well mix. Management committed to net-zero operational emissions by 2050 with interim targets including methane emissions intensity reduction by 2025, zero routine flaring by 2025, and near-zero methane intensity by 2030.
uncertainty_profile: Evaluating impacts of Inflation Reduction Act including minimum tax on book income and excise tax on share repurchases, though impacts not expected to be material. Port Arthur LNG project subject to concluding definitive agreements, engineering contracts, financing and final investment decision. Libya production subject to periodic force majeure events while evolving climate change legislation could materially impact results.
